Back
Principal’s Message
Home
Principal’s Message
Principal's Message
Dr. C.M. Jadhao
M.E.(Etx Engg), Ph.D.(Etx Engg), IETE (Fellow)
It is my privilege to warmly welcome you on the portals of this great institution dedicated to the cause of top-quality technical education in the spiritual land of Shree Sant Gajanan Maharaj and our institute is run by Late Purushottam Hari (Ganesh) Patil Shikshan Sanstha (LPHGPSS) has been founded by Late Gajanandada P Patil, in the memory of Late Purushottam Hari (Ganesh) Patil, and run by Honorable Chairman Shree Dnyaneshwardada Patil and Shree Pandurangdada Patil both of whom firmly believed that value-based quality education is the best foundation for success in life. With this in mind, at Mauli College of Engineering and Technology, we focus on excellent infrastructure and faculty for imparting the highest quality of holistic education and emphasize inculcating the right values through discipline and spiritual education.
In the very first year college, has earned a reputation and credibility that takes decades for other professional institutes to establish. College is still in its infancy stage. We, the management, the teaching faculty, the administrative staff and the students, who form a complete team, have to form the traditions and culture of the college. I am sure that in the future students will not only live up to those traditions, discipline of the college but also excel in every field to make this college “A center for excellence”. In coming years, I am confident that this college will produce “Professionals with a Difference”. The holistic approach of the institute stimulates innovation among students by inspiring fresh ideas with different perceptiveness, creative thinking and strong conviction to achieve true success. We set very high academic standards. Students come here mainly from the rural and economical poor background and want to do their best. They work incredibly hard to achieve not only their hopes and dreams, but also the hopes and dreams of their families. Together, the culture we create is one of success. We are trying our level best towards providing holistic and quality education by infusing each and every student with the zeal to be passionate about learning as well as to be engaged and inspired. We want every student to be nurtured into strong, reflective and responsive individuals who are confident and adaptable in meeting the challenges and opportunities of this rapidly changing world. We are having a very beautiful and eco friendly campus, thus it has a touch of a home away from home feeling hostel facility for boys and girls and also soon we are developing a residential campus for staff as well.
Our examination results are remarkable and tie up with many companies for placement.
I, take this opportunity to thank all the stakeholders for showing interest and continuous support in achieving pioneering position. I extend my best wishes to all students in their chosen career path.

Perspective Plan Of Institute
The institute is dedicated to ideal of providing quality education to the rural backward area that can be compared with the quality of education given in urban sophisticated area. Institute has the perspective plan to achieve its vision.
Short Term Goals
To introduce post graduate courses in Mechanical engineering and Computer Science & Engineering.
Induction of more number of Ph.D. faculties.
Enrichment of ICT based teaching-learning processes.
Obtaining grants for research projects from various Government funding agencies and patents.
Explore collaborative research projects and consultancy with the industry, NGO and Governmental organizations.
Plan for extensions in the building, and renovations to meet the growth requirements.
To strengthen student placement in national and multi-national companies.
To introduce industry relevant programs in association with leading industries through tie-ups.
NAAC and NBA accreditation.
Long Term Goals
Accreditation by international organizations.
Establishment of centers of excellence in various technology domains with industry support.
Collaborations with foreign universities.
To get copyrights and patents on the research work.

Computer Science & Engineering
DEPARTMENT OF COMPUTER SCIENCE AND ENGINEERING
Vision
To provide quality education in Computer Science and Engineering with research that responds swiftly to the ever changing needs of society.
Mission
• To produce qualified and motivated graduates through curriculum and research in the era of Computing.
• To create ambiance to enhance problem solving skills, leadership qualities and team spirit.
• To enhance global exposure of the students through academic and industrial collaborations.
ABOUT DEPARTMENT
Prof. (Dr.) A. S. Maneker
Head, Department of Computer Science and Engineering,
MGI-COET, Shegaon
I welcome all the students & their parents to the Department of Computer Science and Engineering. Computer Science and Engineering is an essence of today’s world. We are committed to providing not only the technical education to our students but also the leadership qualities through which they can create employment to others.
The Department of CSE is striving hard towards the goal of providing innovative and quality education with high standard to achieve academic excellence and provides platform for the students to achieve their career goals.
The Department has a team of highly experienced and motivated faculty members who are in the process of tuning the young minds to make them globally competitive. Innovative methods of teaching and learning process are adopted to achieve learning abilities through practice, exposure and motivation. Department has an excellent infrastructural and computing facilities and provides a conducive environment to promote academic and research excellence in the department. Above all, the entire teaching fraternity of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering look forward to the holistic growth of the department and they aim at doing something extraordinarily remarkable in the academic arena of Computer Science and Engineering.
Department also has a ‘Computer Science and Engineering Student Association’ (CSESA) which provides a platform for students so that they can explore their hidden skills and developed their overall personality.

Electronic & Telecomm
DEPARTMENT ELECTRONICS AND TELECOMMUNICATION ENGINEERING
Vision
To provide technical education with research in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering to meet the social and global needs.
Mission
• To achieve academic excellence through interactive teaching learning process with research.
• To produce resourceful and responsible engineers through comprehensive training programmes to serve the society.
HOD`s MESSAGE
Prof. (Dr.) S. S. Mhaske
Head, Department of Electronics And Telecommunication Engineering,
MGI-COET, Shegaon
Mob.No.-9421295017
The ENTC department works with the objective of addressing critical challenges faced by the Industry, society, and academia. The department runs one UG (BE-EXTC) and one PG (ME-DE) program to cater to the ever-changing needs of technical excellence in all areas of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering such as communication, Embedded Systems, Signal Processing, etc. The alumni of the department are well-placed in reputed government and private firms. The department has a blend of senior, young, and dynamic staff.  The department has well-stocked laboratories having various modern equipment and advanced software to enable the students to keep pace with the fast-changing technology.
Department laboratories are:
Electronics Devices and Circuit Laboratory.
Communication Engineering Laboratory.
Digital Integrated Circuits Laboratory.
Microprocessor and Microcontroller Laboratory.
Digital Signal Processing Laboratory.
ENTC Research Laboratory.
Embedded System Design Laboratory.

Electrical Engineering
Home
Electrical Engineering
DEPARTMENT OF ELECTRICAL ENGINEERING
Vision
To produce globally acceptable electrical engineers through technical education and research for the benefit of society.
Mission
• To provide quality education in the field of electrical engineering.
• To contribute in recent technological advancement to meet the need of Industry and society.
HOD`s MESSAGE
Prof. (Dr.) M. A. Beg
Head, Department of Electrical (Electronics and Power) Engineering,
MGI-COET,Shegaon
Mob.No.-9860232968
Welcome to the Department of Electrical Engineering of MGICOET Shegaon !
Electrical engineers design the most sophisticated systems ever built. From computers with billions of transistors to micro grids fed by renewable energy sources, from algorithms that predict disease to solar cells and electric vehicles, electrical engineering touches all parts of modern society.
The Department of Electrical Engineering at MGICOET offers a vibrant environment for Under Graduate (BE) and Post Graduate (ME) education in Electrical Engineering. The vision of the department is to produce globally acceptable Electrical engineers through technical education and research for the benefit of society.
The Department is, running UG program in Electrical (Electronics and Power) Engineering ,PG program in Electrical Power Systems and PhD program in Electrical Engineering.
The Department has experienced and  well qualified faculty and staff. The Department has adequately equipped laboratories like Basic Electrical lab, Electrical Machines lab, Switchgear and Protection lab, Network Analysis lab, Control System lab and Project lab to meet the requirement of curriculum. The lab facilities are regularly upgraded to provide opportunities for the students to study recent technological advancement in industry and innovate.
The department regularly organizes training programmes, Expert lectures and workshops for the faculty and students to prepare the students for absorption by the industry. All undergraduate and postgraduate students thereby get full opportunity to participate in co-curricular and extra-curricular activities leading to a holistic personality development.
Faculty members of our department are not only very passionate about teaching but are also engaged in research. We pride ourselves on having students in University merit list every year. We provide a very informal atmosphere between faculty and students.
Electrical Department is a place where faculty and students come together to develop new ideas and projects.
The department is committed to advancement of the frontiers of knowledge in Electrical Engineering and to provide the students with a stimulating and rewarding learning experience.

Head, Department of Electrical (Electronics and Power) Engineering,

Home
Mechanical Engineering
DEPARTMENT OF MECHANICAL ENGINEERING
Vision
To be leading Mechanical Engineering Department producing globally competent engineers to serve the society
Mission
• To impart quality education to produce skilled and employable graduates.
• To prepare students for research, entrepreneurship and interdisciplinary team work with ethical values and social responsibility.
• To develop professional potential of faculty, staff and students through collaboration with industry, academia and alumni.
HOD`s MESSAGE
Prof.(Dr.) Rahul B. Ingle
Head, Department of Mechanical Engineering,
MGI-COET,Shegaon
Mob.No.-97307 52607
Mechanical engineering Dept has well setup labs such as Engineering mechanics, fluid power, energy conversion, metallurgical lab, Mechanics of material lab, engineering workshop, machine shop etc.
Mechanical Engineering is one of the broadest engineering disciplines. Mechanical engineers use the principles of energy, materials, and mechanics to design and manufacture machines and devices of all types. Engineers in this discipline work onpower-producing machines such as electric generators,internal combustion engines, and steam and gas turbines. They also work on power-using machines such as refrigeration and air-conditioning equipment, machine tools, material handling systems, elevators and escalators, industrialproduction equipment, and robots used in manufacturing.Mechanical engineers also design tools that other engineers need for their work.Analysis, design, and synthesis are the key functions of mechanical engineers. The question is often how devices and processes actually work. The first step is to visualize what is happening and clearly state the problem. A mechanical engineer will then use computer-based modeling,simulation, and visualization techniques to test different solutions.
Vision of the department includes inculcating the principles of mechanical engineering intothe students using modern teaching-aids. State of the art laboratories, workshops are planned to cater the need of the nearby industries, agricultural populace etc. Academicians, industry-experts are invited on routine basisto address the students and the nearby industrial technocrats. Surrounding populace is invited to view various Projects prepared by the students, staff etc. Mission of the depart well as educating the nearby populace towards the modernization in all the respects.

Civil Engineering
Home
Civil Engineering
DEPARTMENT OF CIVIL ENGINEERING
Vision
To contribute effectively to the society by producing competent professionals in the field of Civil Engineering keeping pace with research and development.
Mission
• To develop competent professionals in the field of design and development of civil engineering structures.
• To promote research and development activities through interaction with the industries.
• To impart soft skills, entrepreneurial skills and ethical values among the students.
HOD`s MESSAGE
Prof.(Dr.) Mukesh Khedkar
Head, Department of Civil Engineering,
MGI-COET, Shegaon
Mob.No.-9022375508
Welcome to the evergreen branch of engineering and a warm welcome to the Department of Civil Engineering, Mauli Group of Institutions, College of Engineering and Technology, Shegaon!
Since its establishment, the civil engineering department has taken a massive jump to be recognized amongst the best and major engineering departments in the region. The department is up-to-date with the high end laboratories in various streams of civil engineering. Faculties having M.E / M.Tech and Ph.D. qualifications benefit undergraduate students building their civil engineering backbone. As evident from the several technical paper publications every year, the department has a good hold on research and consultancy.
The department is actively contributing to the society particularly, the students have been always keen to address real life problems faced by nearby towns and societies through their undergraduate projects. Students of the department are always enthusiastic in representing the institute at various regional and national level competitions.

Department of Applied Sciences and Humanities plays a vital role in an engineering college catering to the teaching of basic Sciences and Humanities courses for engineering students of all branches. The purpose of applied sciences in Engineering study is to lay a strong foundation of basic principles of various disciplines such as Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Computers, Electronics, Mechanics and Communication Skills in the mind of the learners, so that they proceed to rest of their years of study with up to date knowledge and training of basic engineering skills.
Department is backed by well-educated, talented and dedicated faculty members to fulfill this task. In addition to the primary teaching duties discharged by them, many of them have their publications in National as well as International Journals of repute for attaining perfection in their profession. The subjects taught by teachers of this department are of prime importance for all round growth and development of students.
The students are given personal attention and care by monitoring their academic performance, by conducting improvement classes through peer guided self-study methodology, tutorial classes and regular counseling. Home assignments, quizzes & viva-voce are regularly assigned and conducted as a part of teaching-learning process and to prepare students to face the internal & external examinations. Environmental studies make our students stewards of the environment that sustains us, our families and communities now and in the future. Physics, Chemistry Laboratories have been very well established and well equipped with all the modern gadgets and instruments. Department not only focus to cover the complete syllabus but to motivate students to learn beyond the syllabus which definitely develops complete knowledge of the subject (both the practical and theoretical depth of knowledge).
The Language laboratory is an integral part of the department. It has been established with a definitive objective to improve upon both oral and writing skill through the use of Computers and is in use for language study activities. Valuing the importance of international language, the college has established the Language Communication Laboratory with the mission of imparting training to twenty students at a time. Students get sufficient exposure considering the practical aspect of their learning & training. Moreover, it is planned to impart additional training in different languages such as French and Spanish from next session. Open group discussions and debate are being conducted in the Laboratory. The teacher works as a facilitator during all this process. Really, the lab has proved tremendously in shaping the students basic skills of listening attentively and speaking fluently.
Last, but not the least, the department is deeply engaged into many development activities that are essential for the overall development of students. These activities involve sports & cultural events, club events, etc.

Campus Facilities
Campus Facilities
RO Water Plant
UV-RO Water plant is available within the campus for constant supply of safe drinking water
Purified water is supplied to all academic blocks, canteen and hostels.
Storage capacity: 2000 liters SS container
Filtration Capacity: 1000 liters/hr
Treatment: UV – RO treated oxidized
This Reverse Osmosis Plant caters to the drinking water needs of all the Students, Teachers, supporting Staff and the Visitors. The raw water with an average Total Dissolved Solids [TDS] of 800-1000 ppm is treated to reduce the TDSF content to less than 100 ppm the generally acceptable upper limit of the TDS.
DG set
The institute is supported with 24X7 power back up by a dedicated DG set of capacity 5KVA. It helps the students to stay comfortable and study uninterruptedly. This facilitates class rooms, Library, Laboratories, Canteen, Seminar Hall, hostels & all sections of campus.
A UPS of rating 10kVA is devoted for the computer labs and five UPS of rating 1kVA each are employed for Principal and HOD’s rooms.
We strive to provide our students a comfortable, quality and secure stay in the campus.
Solar Power Plant
As a part of green campus initiatives to make the institute a sustainable and eco-friendly, in its beginning phase only MGI-COET has installed a roof top solar power plant with a capacity of 40 kwp. This was truly significant milestone. It is “On Grid” type of PV solar power plant. The power generated during holidays and excess power in working days is fed back to grid. It consist of 136 solar plates with rating of 295W each. This plant generate approximately 160 kwh per day catering 50% of energy requirement of the institute. This is equivalent to reduction of 3,035kg of carbon per month.  There are two inverters 20 KW each connected to this solar power plant. Along with the Solar Generation meter, Net meter is also connected here in the institute. This Net meter is the first net meter installed in the Buldhana district.

Anti Ragging
ANTI - RAGGING
Mauli Group of Institution’s, College of Engineering and Technology, Shegaon strictly prohibits ragging in its campus. A strict anti-ragging policy is designed to prohibit, prevent and eliminate the menace of ragging in the institute. If anyone is found engaging in any form of ragging, he/she will face strict action, regardless of the extent of their involvement. To prevent such activities, an anti-ragging committee has been formed and it is actively operational under the direction of Hon’ble Principal.
Objectives :
To eliminate ragging in all its forms from the institution and punish those who indulge in ragging as per the appropriate law in force.
To ensure that a safe and welcoming environment is maintained on the campus and to protect the students from any form of ragging.
Anti Ragging Policy at MGI-COET, Shegaon :
In pursuance to the judgement of Hon’ble Supreme Court of India (Civil Appeal No. 887/2009 dated 08-05-2009), ragging is a criminal offence and is totally prohibited in the campus.
If anyone found guilty of ragging and/or abetting ragging, whether actively or passively, or being a part of conspiracy to promote ragging, is liable to be punished in accordance with the regulations as framed by the University Grants Commission (UGC) vide Gazette Notification No. F.1-16/2007 (CPP-II) dated 17-06-2009 of the Government of India and Regulation Councils as well as under the provision of any penal law for the time being in force.
Institutional Initiatives to promote ragging free campus :
Monitoring by CCTV Cameras:
CCTV cameras are placed in order to monitor student activities at vital points in the campus and hostel premises.
Anti-ragging Undertaking:
As per the instructions of the government, Anti-ragging Undertakings are taken from all students at the time of admission. The submission of the document ensures the students’ commitment of compliance with the government’s policy of eradicating ragging.
Organizing Awareness Programs:
Awareness Programs are regularly organized to educate the students about the ills of Ragging and the need to curb it at all levels. In these programs, detail information is given about the serious consequences of ragging and the legal measures to be followed to get rid of ragging.
Anti-ragging Warning Boards in the College Premises:
Anti-ragging warning boards are placed at different locations in the campus for the information of students and parents.

RESEARCH PROMOTION CELL (RPC)
In order to promote research and development activities at MGICOET, a Research Promotion Cell (RPC) has been constituted. The mandate of RPC is to disseminate information, facilitate the process and encourage the students and faculty to incorporate research activities in their academic training.
The Endeavour of Research Promotion Cell would be to:
•   Promote quality research in the all the disciplines;
•   Disseminate information about research project grants;
•   Facilitate sending of research proposals to research funding organizations;
•   Organize training programme for capacity building of teachers for applying and conducting research projects;
•   Extend hassle free administrative support for completing the Research projects;
•   Emphasize on IPR activities, research publications in Journals and paper presentations in reputed conferences and seminars;
•   Motivate the faculty to pursue research degrees and post-doctoral research;
•   Monitor ongoing research projects in the institution;
•   Encourage the faculty for technology transfer and to support them.

IIC is an Initiative of the Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD), Govt. of India for selected Higher Education Institutes.
MIC & IIC
MHRD has established ‘MHRD’s own Innovation Cell (MIC)’. The primary mandate of MIC is to encourage, inspire and nurture young students by supporting them to work with new ideas and transform their idea into prototypes, while they are in formative years.
MIC plans to achieve this by systematically fostering the culture of Innovation amongst all Higher Education Institutions (HEIs), by creating individual ‘Institution’s Innovation Council (IICs)’ across selected HEIs.
A network of these IICs will be established to promote innovation in the Institution through multitudinous modes leading to an innovation promotion eco-system in the campuses.
Mauli Group Of Institution`s College of Engineering and Technology, Shegaon.is a member of MHRD’s Innovation Cell (MIC). The institution’s Innovation Council
(IIC)

Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE)
Introduction
The Indian Society for Technical Education (ISTE) is the leading National Professional non-profit making Society for the Technical Education System in our country with the motto of Career Development of Teachers and Personality Development of Students and overall development of our Technical Education System. Being the only national organisation of educators in the field of Engineering and Technology, ISTE effectively contributes in various missions of the Union Government. The strength of ISTE is its strong base in technical education institutions in the country.
Objectives
The major objective of the ISTE is to provide quality training programs to teachers and administrators of technical institutions to update their knowledge and skills in their fields of activity and to assist and contribute in the production and development of top-quality professional engineers and technicians needed by the industry and other organizations.
Activities
It organizes an annual convention for faculty and students separately every year where a large number of technocrats, technical teachers, policymakers, experts from the industry etc. participate and interact.
Benefits
Every year a National Seminar with a specific theme with respect to the latest development in the field of Science and Technology and societal problems is being arranged during the Annual convention and leading luminaries of technical education are invited to deliver special lectures and delegates will present research papers. ISTE is actively involved in many activities conducted by the All India Council for Technical Education New Delhi (AICTE) and the National Board of Accreditation New Delhi (NBA). ISTE is having collaboration and MoUs with government organizations and various Universities for the promotion of technical education system in the country.

National Service Scheme (NSS)
About National Service Scheme (NSS)
The National Service Scheme (NSS) is an Indian government-sponsored public service program conducted by the Department of Youth Affairs and Sports of the Government of India. Popularly known as NSS, the scheme was launched in Gandhiji’s Centenary year, 1969. Aimed at developing student’s personality through community service, NSS is a voluntary association of young people in Colleges, Universities and at +2 level working for a campus-community linkage.
In India, the idea of involving students in the task of national service dates back to the times of Mahatma Gandhi, the father of the nation. The central theme which he tried to impress upon his student audience time and again was that they should always keep before them, their social responsibility. The first duty of the students should be, not to treat their period of study as one of the opportunities for indulgence in intellectual luxury, but for preparing themselves for final dedication in the service of those who provided the sinews of the nation with the national goods & services so essential to society. Advising them to form a living contact with the community in whose midst their institution is located, he suggested that instead of undertaking academic research about economic and social disability, the students should do “something positive so that the life of the villagers might be raised to a higher material and moral level”.
Objective:
(i) To understand the community in which they work.
(ii) To understand themselves in relation to their community.
(iii) To identify the needs and problems of the community and involve them in problem solving process.
(iv) To develop among themselves a sense of social and civic responsibility.
(v) To utilize their knowledge in finding practical solution to individual and community problems.
(vi) To develop competence required for group-living and sharing of responsibilities:
(vii) To gain skills in mobilizing community participation.
(viii) To acquire leadership qualities and democratic attitude.
(ix) To develop capacity to meet emergencies and natural disasters.
(x) To practice national integration and social harmony.
Activities:
1. National Yoga Day
2. Tree-Plantation
3. Blood Donation
4. Shramdan

CODE OF CONDUCT
Home
CODE OF CONDUCT
Preamble
MGI-COET, Shegaon has its own core values and practices which are considered to be fundamental and essential to study and practice of engineering. The institute maintains high standards of honor and integrity in professional development of both, students and staff. The code of conduct prepared by the institute serves the guideline to maintain ethical standards and it supports the institutional framework for achieving the Vision. It is expected that all the teaching, non-teaching staff and students should strictly adhere to the code of conduct mentioned in this document.
Institute believes that the code is a road map towards our mission and vision, so every employee should, ‘Know it and Live it’. The employee should read and understand this code so that they might readily distinguish any proposal or act that would constitute a violation. Each employee is responsible for his/her actions. Violations of this Code will lead to disciplinary action for employees including termination.
Demonstrating Personal Integrity
1. Smoking, Drug and Alcohol Free Workplace Policy:
The possession, distribution and the use of illegal drugs/alcohol and smoking by employees and students of the institute is strictly prohibited as per the institute policy. An employee/student convicted for violation of the policy would face a strong disciplinary action which may include termination of employment with NO further notice and intimation and suspending enrollment of student from the college.
2. Gratuities:
Employees of the Institute do not accept gratuities, courtesies, or gifts in any form from any person or persons, corporations, or associations that, directly or indirectly, seek to use the connection so as to secure favorable comment or consideration on any commercial commodity, process or undertaking.
3. Human Rights, Diversity, Preventing Sexual harassment and Violence in the Workplace:
Institute is committed to conducting all of its affairs with fairness and equity and fostering a unique and inclusive culture by providing a safe and respectful work environment that is free from harassment, discrimination, violence and other unwelcome behavior. In support of this commitment:
• Institute to give equal opportunity to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to religion, race, creed, caste, color, sex, disability, and age; if the person is qualified and able to perform the “essential functions” of the job with “reasonable accommodation.”
• Institute will not tolerate any harassment on any ground. Sexual harassment of employees or students such as unwelcome sexual advancements, requests for sexual favors, and other verbal or physical conduct of sexual nature at the Institute is prohibited and offender is dismissed or other disciplinary action is taken.
• Institute will not tolerate or ignore any kind of violence.
• Employee/student must immediately report any harassing, discriminatory or violent conduct, unsafe working conditions or any on-the-job-injury so that it may be properly addressed.
4. Confidential Information:
Some employees handle confidential information as they perform their duties. Such information is not to be discussed or made available to anyone until it has been approved for release by proper authority. Any person found using such classified information invites suitable disciplinary action against him / her depending on the severity of the matter.
5. Conflicts of Interest:
An employee of the Institute avoids actual or apparent conflicts of interest between his/ her institute’s obligations/ responsibilities and outside activities.
6. Attendance and Dress code:
Employees are to be prompt in reporting to work on time in proper dress code decided by the institute. It is expected that employees will remain on the job until the end of the workday unless excused by the Head of the department an employee, who knows he / she will be absent from work on a particular day should report such anticipated absence to the Head of the Department. A record of tardiness and unexcused absenteeism may result in loss of pay or other disciplinary action.
Duties and Resposibilities: Teachers and Non-Teaching Staff
Teachers should follow the rules and responsibilities as per UGC Regulations, 2010 published in Gazette of India, Sep. 18, 2010, part III, Section 4.
All the staff has to mention his/her signature on the attendance register.
Use of mobile phone while in the class/practical labs is strictly prohibited.
Those desiring to resign or leave the college for any reason must intimate the college regarding this at least one/three month in advance or he/she shall forfeit one/ three month’s salary in lieu of the notice for ad-hoc/approved staff respectively.
All the works assigned to the teacher must be done with honesty and dedication. Any laxity in doing one’s duty shall be intolerable and disciplinary action may be initiated against him/her.
No one may leave the college during college hours without the permission of the head of the department/institution.
No one shall use the name or property of the institute for personal benefit or advantage.
No one shall involve himself/herself in students’ strikes, riots or demonstrations without recourse to the due process.
It is an obligation on a Non-teaching staff upon a request from a teacher, Head of the department/institution or officials, to submit reports and data or information timely without failure.
All employees are governed by the conducts of college and are bound to follow rules and regulations set by the management from time to time.
Duties and Responsibilities: Students
Student must abide by the rules and regulations framed by the institute and Principal from time to time.
Students should come to the college campus in college uniform.
The college is committed to the policy of zero tolerance with regard to harassment, intimidation, and discrimination of any kind of girl students on campus. If, any act of harassment/ intimidation/ discrimination of any kind towards girl students is found, severe penalty will be imposed on the perpetrators of such crimes.
Ragging of the student is offence under Indian penal code. Any student found involved in this act will be punished as per the law
The student is expected to read the notices put up on the notice board of the college regularly. The college is not responsible for any loss or damage caused to the student due to his failure to read the notices from time to time.
Any change in the residential address of the student must be communicated to the office immediately.
Student should carry their identity card while in the college.
Teasing/harassment of fellow students are strictly prohibited in the college campus. Strict disciplinary action will be taken against students involved in such activities.
Possession or consumption of narcotic drugs, tobacco, alcohol and other intoxicating substances are strictly prohibited in the campus and hostels. MGI-COET campus is a “smoking free campus”.
Students should drive safely inside the campus as well as outside the campus.
Student should not cause any damage to the institute’s property. The damage caused shall be recovered from student immediately.
The student should have minimum 75 % attendance in each course.
Students should keep the class room tidy. They should switch off lights and fans when they are not required.
If the student is found weak in certain subject he/she will have to attend extra classes as notified and make the required progress to come at par with the other students.
In case, a student remain absent for some reason, he/she will be required to take the prior permission of the head of the Department / Class counselor.
Malpractices during all examination and tests will be taken seriously and disciplinary action will be taken against the defaulters.
Students shall not entertain visitors without prior permission in the campus.
Students are not allowed to convene any kind of meeting in the campus or any sort of fund collection without the permission of the HOD/ Academic Coordinator/Principal.
Students who plagiarize may be considered to be guilty of academic misconduct.
Silence shall be maintained in the academic premises of the college. Student’s behavior which interfere with teaching-learning process will not be tolerated.
Any student failing to observe this code of conduct, will be liable to the punishment decided by the college administration depending upon the gravity of the misconduct or its recurrence.

Continuous Internal Evaluation (CIE)
The evaluation of the students is an integral part of the teaching-learning process. The Continuous Internal evaluation (CIE):
Encourages the students to progress continuously in the semester leading to thorough understanding of the course.
Allows teachers to evaluate the performance of their students in accordance with the course objectives.
Enhances the students’ abilities and skills such as creative and critical thinking, working in a team, communication skill, leadership abilities, etc.
Distributes course work throughout the semester reducing stress on the students.
